name: La Serena Reservoir and Cima de las Poyatas Loop
name_original: Embalse de la Serena y Alto de las Poyatas – circular desde Peñalsordo
description: This difficult 36.3-mile mountain biking route near Peñalsordo offers challenging climbs and stunning views of Embalse de la Serena.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Peñalsordo offers diverse terrain characterized by the vast Embalse de la Serena reservoir and distinctive hills. The region features varied landscapes, from challenging dirt roads to areas with significant elevation changes. Conical hills like Cerro Masatrigo provide panoramic views, while the presence of the reservoir shapes many of the longer routes. The area's natural features create a dynamic environment for mountain bikers seeking different levels of challenge. Best mountain bike trails around Peñalsordo The most popular mountain bike trail is La Serena Reservoir and Cima de las Poyatas Loop, a 36.4 miles (58.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Capilla and its Castle – Castillo de Capilla loop from Cabeza Del Buey, a difficult 32.4 miles (52.2 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs and descends through varied landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Collado de Santa Ana loop from Zarza-Capilla, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail leading through hilly terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Peñalsordo is defined by large reservoirs, prominent hills, and a network of dirt roads, offering options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Peñalsordo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Peñalsordo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the typical difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Peñalsordo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Peñalsordo primarily cater to moderate and difficult skill levels. There are 2 moderate routes and 3 difficult routes available, offering challenges with significant elevation changes and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Peñalsordo?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Peñalsordo are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033015064/la-serena-reservoir-and-cima-de-las-poyatas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Reservoir and Cima de las Poyatas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 36.4-mile ride, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148128" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Santa Ana loop from Zarza-Capilla&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 25.5-mile trail.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails in Peñalsordo?
A: The region is rich in natural landmarks. You can encounter the vast Embalse de la Serena (La Serena Reservoir) on many routes. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, known as 'the magic mountain' for its conical shape, offers wonderful 360-degree views. Another impressive rock formation is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt;, which is also a great spot for birdwatching.

Q: What is the terrain like for mountain biking in Peñalsordo?
A: The terrain in Peñalsordo is diverse, characterized by the vast Embalse de la Serena reservoir and distinctive hills. You'll find varied landscapes, from challenging dirt roads, such as the CO-9027 Dirt Road, to areas with significant elevation changes. Conical hills like Cerro Masatrigo contribute to the dynamic environment, providing both scenic beauty and demanding climbs.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by mountain bike in the area?
A: Yes, you can include historical sites in your ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31458641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capilla and its Castle – Castillo de Capilla loop from Cabeza Del Buey&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 32.4-mile route that takes you past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Capilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Peñalsordo?
A: While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, the region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would offer the most comfortable temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be very hot, which might make longer, more difficult routes challenging. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Peñalsordo?
A: The routes in Peñalsordo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Mountain bikers often praise the challenging routes, the breathtaking panoramic views from elevated points like Cerro Masatrigo, and the diverse ecosystems that provide a dynamic environment for both adventure and scenic immersion.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails near Peñalsordo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Peñalsordo vary in length. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033015064/la-serena-reservoir-and-cima-de-las-poyatas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Reservoir and Cima de las Poyatas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 36.4 miles (58.5 km) and takes approximately 5 hours 20 minutes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527394" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de las Piedras loop from Sancti-Spíritus&lt;/a&gt;, which is 23.9 miles (38.4 km) long.

Q: Are there any particularly long or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a significant challenge can tackle the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza de Buey – Capilla and its Castle loop from Cabeza Del Buey&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans 37.9 miles (61.1 km) and features substantial elevation gain, making it a demanding ride for those seeking a longer adventure.

Q: Are there any specific dirt roads recommended for mountain biking in the area?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4289272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CO-9027 Dirt Road&lt;/a&gt;, extending from Santa Eufemia to Estación de Belalcázar, is specifically noted as an ideal route for mountain biking and gravel cycling. Its challenging condition, with numerous potholes, is considered an advantage for a fun and engaging ride, offering beautiful landscapes throughout.
tips: Beautiful area to ride a mountain bike near the Embalse de la Serena. We must bear in mind that it is an area for hunters and during hunting seasons we must be careful. (or avoid the area)

Beautiful views from the Cima de las Poyatas, located at 856 meters above sea level in the Sierra del Torozo. During our climb to the top, we may have to push the bike because of its inclination and difficulty.

faqs.question: What level of fitness and skill is required for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as a "Hard mountain bike ride." It demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for challenging climbs, descents, and potentially technical sections where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Embalse de la Serena y Alto de las Poyatas route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. While specific details for this route are not fully outlined, the general area suggests a mix of unpaved paths, potentially rocky or loose sections, and steep climbs and descents. The difficulty rating indicates you should expect technical portions.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning natural beauty, primarily centered around the vast Embalse de la Serena, one of Europe's largest reservoirs. The route offers expansive "inland sea" vistas and traverses Extremaduran pasturelands featuring holm oaks, cork oaks, heather, juniper, and rockrose. It's also part of a Special Protection Area for Birds, offering opportunities for wildlife observation.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes significant points of interest. You will pass by Cerro de Majada Vieja and the Cima de las Poyatas (856 m), both offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape and the reservoir.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Peñalsordo. You should look for suitable parking options within or near the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the region's climate, the best times to ride this trail are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are generally milder and more pleasant for demanding physical activity, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in natural areas allow dogs, it's always best to check local regulations for the specific area around Embalse de la Serena, especially since it's a Special Protection Area for Birds. If dogs are permitted, they should be kept on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on a challenging mountain bike route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails in this region. However, as the trail traverses a Special Protection Area for Birds (La Serena and Perier Sierras), always respect environmental regulations and stay on marked paths to protect the local ecosystem.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route does intersect with other paths. Approximately 11% of the route follows sections of the Camino del Condado de Belalcázar - Etapa 3, and a smaller portion (1%) coincides with the Sendero del Peñón de Pez.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or insider tips for tackling this route?
faqs.answer: The primary challenge is the route's significant length (58.5 km) and substantial elevation gain (1,160 meters), requiring sustained effort. An insider tip would be to start early in the day, especially during warmer months, to avoid the midday heat and allow ample time to complete the ride, as it's estimated to take over 5 hours. Ensure your bike is in excellent condition and carry sufficient water and nutrition.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodations near the trailhead in Peñalsordo?
faqs.answer: Peñalsordo is a local town, so you can generally expect to find basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and potentially some local accommodation options. It's advisable to check in advance for specific opening hours or availability, especially if you plan to stay overnight or need services before/after your ride.
